what_it_does: This file is a living noticeboard guide that documents how to launch Claude Code sessions and clones against company Ollama models using Ollama's native Anthropic endpoint on :11434, rather than routing through the litellm :4100 proxy. It gives interactive and programmatic invocation forms, lists cloud and local model tags, points to an existing local concurrent directory-ingest capability, and explains the rationale and a stale-service cleanup note.
observations: The first version wrongly routed through the litellm :4100 proxy; this corrected version routes directly to Ollama :11434.
Ollama speaks Anthropic-native, so Claude Code can use ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434 with ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY empty.
kimi/glm models appeared broken through litellm because litellm translation returned empty content, not because the models are broken.
The litellm :4100 proxy is load-bearing for the company's own fabric cognition and should be left alone.
The stale leftover to clean up is ~/claude-gateway (:4000), referenced at services.json:562.
Interactive launcher: ollama launch claude --model <tag> -- <claude flags>; headless requires --model before the --.
Programmatic/supervisor path exports ANTHROPIC_* env vars and runs claude --model <tag>.
SUP.spawn(provider='ollama', model='<tag>') and cc_clone.clone_at(..., provider='ollama', model='<tag>') inject these env vars.
Cloud model tags include kimi-k2.7-code:cloud, deepseek-v4-flash:cloud, deepseek-v4-pro:cloud, glm-5.1:cloud, minimax-m3:cloud, qwen3.5:397b-cloud.
Local free GPU-loaded tags include qwen3.5-9b-q8:latest, qwen3.6-35b-a3b-iq3s:latest, gemma4-26b-a4b-q3km:latest.
mcp__company__ingest (run_items) already provides free concurrent directory scanning using the local 4B on :8000.
The change builds on existing ollama-cloud setup, spawn --model, and ingest; only the ANTHROPIC_* env seam was added.
standouts: The central correction: Claude Code must go direct to Ollama :11434, not through the litellm :4100 proxy.
Verified launch commands and env form for kimi-k2.7-code:cloud.
Supervisor and clone integration now uses provider='ollama' to inject the native Anthropic env seam.
Pointer to existing free local concurrent directory scans via mcp__company__ingest.
conventions: Follows the channel-memory noticeboard YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, state, history, timestamps, and author_session.
Uses uppercase section headers and command-line examples.
References internal artifacts by path and line number (fabric/config.py, services.json:562).
Distinguishes interactive vs headless CLI shapes with the -- separator.
Model tags are given with exact suffixes (:cloud, :latest).
concerns: The stale ~/claude-gateway (:4000) cleanup is mentioned but no concrete cleanup steps are given.
Verification is stated only for kimi-k2.7-code:cloud, yet the guide generalizes to many other cloud and local tags.
Setting ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="" may interact unexpectedly with other Anthropic-aware tooling in the same environment.
The guide mixes operational runbook, architectural rationale, and cleanup note, which may drift independently.
notes: This item is the corrected runbook for launching Claude Code against company Ollama models natively on :11434, covering interactive use, supervisor spawns, clones, model tags, and the existing local directory-ingest scanner.
questions: What exactly is at services.json:562 and how should ~/claude-gateway be safely removed?
What is the precise content of fabric/config.py LITELLM_PROXY and how is it used by fabric cognition?
How does the GPU-sequence with the steward work for local models?
Do all listed local models actually support >=64k context in this setup?
What is the exact interface/arguments for mcp__company__ingest (run_items)?
Does ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="" cause failures if other Anthropic clients are present in the same shell?
What makes kimi-k2.7-code:cloud 'Tim's preferred default' and is that preference encoded elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the stated HOW-TO purpose matches the content, which provides launch recipes for CC sessions and clones on company models via Ollama-native. It also adds design rationale and a cleanup note that extend slightly beyond a pure runbook.
apparent_themes: Prefer native endpoints over unnecessary proxies.
Protect load-bearing existing systems from being repurposed.
Verify operational instructions on a real model before generalizing.
Reuse existing infrastructure rather than building new translation layers.
Optimize cost and scale by mixing cheap cloud models with free local GPU inference.
Keep documentation living and explicitly correct prior mistakes.
intention_signals: The file is trying to prevent Claude Code from being misrouted through the litellm fabric proxy, establish the direct Ollama-native Anthropic endpoint as the supported path, and enable cheaper cloud and free local model usage for both interactive sessions and automated supervisor/clones, while surfacing an already-built local directory-scan capability.
novelty: It is a corrected noticeboard guide that explicitly retracts a prior proxy route, treats Ollama itself as a native Anthropic endpoint for Claude Code, and maps supervisor/clones to an environment-variable seam rather than a separate proxy or adapter.
contribution: It contributes the canonical operational recipe for connecting Claude Code to company Ollama models, the clear boundary between the load-bearing litellm fabric proxy and the CC-native Ollama path, verified interactive and programmatic invocation forms, an inventory of cloud and local model tags, and a pointer to the existing local concurrent directory-ingest capability.
summary_for_embedding: A living noticeboard guide that documents the corrected method to launch Claude Code sessions and clones against company Ollama models via Ollama's native Anthropic endpoint on :11434, listing interactive and programmatic invocation forms, model tags, and an existing local directory-ingest capability.
intention_for_embedding: To stop Claude Code from using the wrong litellm proxy and establish the direct Ollama-native path for cheap cloud and free local model usage across interactive sessions, supervisor spawns, and clones.
